20:class Issue < ApplicationRecord
21:  include Redmine::SafeAttributes
22:  include Redmine::Utils::DateCalculation
23:  include Redmine::I18n
24:
25:  before_validation :default_assign, on: :create
26:  before_validation :force_default_value_on_noneditable_custom_fields, on: :create
27:  before_validation :clear_disabled_fields
28:  before_save :set_parent_id
29:  include Redmine::NestedSet::IssueNestedSet
30:  include Redmine::Reaction::Reactable
31:
32:  belongs_to :project
33:  belongs_to :tracker
34:  belongs_to :status, :class_name => 'IssueStatus'
35:  belongs_to :author, :class_name => 'User'
36:  belongs_to :assigned_to, :class_name => 'Principal'
37:  belongs_to :fixed_version, :class_name => 'Version'
38:  belongs_to :priority, :class_name => 'IssuePriority'
39:  belongs_to :category, :class_name => 'IssueCategory'
40:
41:  has_many :journals, :as => :journalized, :dependent => :destroy, :inverse_of => :journalized
42:  has_many :time_entries, :dependent => :destroy
43:  has_and_belongs_to_many :changesets, lambda {order("#{Changeset.table_name}.committed_on ASC, #{Changeset.table_name}.id ASC")}
44:
45:  has_many :relations_from, :class_name => 'IssueRelation', :foreign_key => 'issue_from_id', :dependent => :delete_all
46:  has_many :relations_to, :class_name => 'IssueRelation', :foreign_key => 'issue_to_id', :dependent => :delete_all
47:
48:  acts_as_attachable :after_add => :attachment_added, :after_remove => :attachment_removed
49:  acts_as_customizable
50:  acts_as_watchable
51:  acts_as_searchable :columns => ['subject', "#{table_name}.description"],
52:                     :preload => [:project, :status, :tracker],
53:                     :scope => lambda {|options| options[:open_issues] ? self.open : self.all}
54:
55:  acts_as_event :title => Proc.new {|o| "#{o.tracker.name} ##{o.id} (#{o.status}): #{o.subject}"},
56:                :url => Proc.new {|o| {:controller => 'issues', :action => 'show', :id => o.id}},
57:                :type => Proc.new {|o| 'issue' + (o.closed? ? '-closed' : '')}
58:
59:  acts_as_activity_provider :scope => proc {preload(:project, :author, :tracker, :status)},
60:                            :author_key => :author_id
61:
62:  acts_as_mentionable :attributes => ['description']
63:  acts_as_webhookable
64:  include Issue::Webhookable
65:
66:  DONE_RATIO_OPTIONS = %w(issue_field issue_status)
67:
68:  attr_reader :transition_warning
69:  attr_writer :deleted_attachment_ids
70:  delegate :notes, :notes=, :private_notes, :private_notes=, :to => :current_journal, :allow_nil => true
71:
72:  validates_presence_of :subject, :project, :tracker
73:  validates_presence_of :priority, :if => Proc.new {|issue| issue.new_record? || issue.priority_id_changed?}
74:  validates_presence_of :status, :if => Proc.new {|issue| issue.new_record? || issue.status_id_changed?}
75:  validates_presence_of :author, :if => Proc.new {|issue| issue.new_record? || issue.author_id_changed?}
76:
77:  validates_length_of :subject, :maximum => 255
78:  validates_inclusion_of :done_ratio, :in => 0..100
79:  validates :estimated_hours, :numericality => {:greater_than_or_equal_to => 0, :allow_nil => true, :message => :invalid}
80:  validates :start_date, :date => true
81:  validates :due_date, :date => true
82:  validate :validate_issue, :validate_required_fields, :validate_permissions
83:
84:  scope :visible, (lambda do |*args|
85:    joins(:project).
86:    where(Issue.visible_condition(args.shift || User.current, *args))
87:  end)
88:
89:  scope :open, (lambda do |*args|
90:    is_closed = !args.empty? ? !args.first : false
91:    joins(:status).
92:    where(:issue_statuses => {:is_closed => is_closed})
93:  end)
94:
95:  scope :recently_updated, lambda {order(:updated_on => :desc)}
96:  scope :on_active_project, (lambda do
97:    joins(:project).
98:    where(:projects => {:status => Project::STATUS_ACTIVE})
99:  end)
100:  scope :fixed_version, (lambda do |versions|
101:    ids = [versions].flatten.compact.map {|v| v.is_a?(Version) ? v.id : v}
102:    ids.any? ? where(:fixed_version_id => ids) : none
103:  end)
104:  scope :assigned_to, (lambda do |arg|
105:    arg = Array(arg).uniq
106:    ids = arg.map {|p| p.is_a?(Principal) ? p.id : p}
107:    ids += arg.grep(User).map(&:group_ids).flatten.uniq
108:    ids.compact!
109:    ids.any? ? where(:assigned_to_id => ids) : none
110:  end)
111:  scope :like, (lambda do |q|
112:    if q.present?
113:      where(*::Query.tokenized_like_conditions("#{table_name}.subject", q))
114:    end
115:  end)
116:
117:  before_save :close_duplicates, :update_done_ratio_from_issue_status,
118:              :force_updated_on_change, :update_closed_on
119:  after_save do |issue|
120:    if !issue.saved_change_to_id? && issue.saved_change_to_project_id?

136:  # Returns a SQL conditions string used to find all issues visible by the specified user
137:  def self.visible_condition(user, options={})
138:    Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_issues, options) do |role, user|
139:      sql =
140:        if user.id && user.logged?
141:          case role.issues_visibility
142:          when 'all'
143:            '1=1'
144:          when 'default'
145:            user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
146:            "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false} " \
147:              "OR #{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} " \
148:              "OR #{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
149:          when 'own'
150:            user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
151:            "(#{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} OR " \
152:              "#{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
153:          else
154:            '1=0'
155:          end
156:        else
157:          "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false})"
158:        end
159:      unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
160:        tracker_ids = role.permissions_tracker_ids(:view_issues)
161:        if tracker_ids.any?
162:          sql = "(#{sql} AND #{table_name}.tracker_id IN (#{tracker_ids.join(',')}))"
163:        else
164:          sql = '1=0'
165:        end
166:      end
167:      sql
168:    end
169:  end

173:    (usr || User.current).allowed_to?(:view_issues, self.project) do |role, user|
174:      visible =
175:        if user.logged?
176:          case role.issues_visibility
177:          when 'all'
178:            true
179:          when 'default'
180:            !self.is_private? || (self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to))
181:          when 'own'
182:            self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to)
183:          else
184:            false
185:          end
186:        else
187:          !self.is_private?
188:        end
189:      unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
190:        visible &&= role.permissions_tracker_ids?(:view_issues, tracker_id)
191:      end
192:      visible
193:    end
194:  end

def initialize(attributes=nil, *args)
    super
    if new_record?
      # set default values for new records only
      self.priority ||= IssuePriority.default
      self.watcher_user_ids = []
    end
  end

  def create_or_update(*args)
    super()
  ensure
    @status_was = nil
  end
  private :create_or_update

  # AR#Persistence#destroy would raise and RecordNotFound exception
  # if the issue was already deleted or updated (non matching lock_version).
  # This is a problem when bulk deleting issues or deleting a project
  # (because an issue may already be deleted if its parent was deleted
  # first).
  # The issue is reloaded by the nested_set before being deleted so
  # the lock_version condition should not be an issue but we handle it.
  def destroy
    super
  rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError, ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    # Stale or already deleted
    begin
      reload
    rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
      # The issue was actually already deleted
      @destroyed = true
      return freeze
    end
    # The issue was stale, retry to destroy
    super
  end

  alias :base_reload :reload
  def reload(*)
    @workflow_rule_by_attribute = nil
    @assignable_versions = nil
    @relations = nil
    @spent_hours = nil
    @total_spent_hours = nil
    @total_estimated_hours = nil
    @last_updated_by = nil
    @last_notes = nil
    base_reload(*)
  end

  # Overrides Redmine::Acts::Customizable::InstanceMethods#available_custom_fields

def update_nested_set_attributes
    if saved_change_to_parent_id?
      update_nested_set_attributes_on_parent_change
    end
    remove_instance_variable(:@parent_issue) if instance_variable_defined?(:@parent_issue)
  end

  # Updates the nested set for when an existing issue is moved
  def update_nested_set_attributes_on_parent_change
    former_parent_id = parent_id_before_last_save
    # delete invalid relations of all descendants
    self_and_descendants.each do |issue|
      issue.relations.each do |relation|
        relation.destroy unless relation.valid?
      end
    end
    # update former parent
    recalculate_attributes_for(former_parent_id) if former_parent_id
  end

  def update_parent_attributes
    if parent_id
      recalculate_attributes_for(parent_id)
      association(:parent).reset
    end
  end

  def recalculate_attributes_for(issue_id)
    if issue_id && p = Issue.find_by_id(issue_id)
      if p.priority_derived?
        # priority = highest priority of open children
        # priority is left unchanged if all children are closed and there's no default priority defined
        if priority_position =
             p.children.open.joins(:priority).maximum("#{IssuePriority.table_name}.position")
          p.priority = IssuePriority.find_by_position(priority_position)
        elsif default_priority = IssuePriority.default
          p.priority = default_priority
        end
      end

      if p.dates_derived?
        # start/due dates = lowest/highest dates of children
        p.start_date = p.children.minimum(:start_date)
        p.due_date = p.children.maximum(:due_date)
        if p.start_date && p.due_date && p.due_date < p.start_date
          p.start_date, p.due_date = p.due_date, p.start_date
        end
      end

      if p.done_ratio_derived?
        # done ratio = average ratio of children weighted with their total estimated hours
        unless Issue.use_status_for_done_ratio? && p.status && p.status.default_done_ratio
          children = p.children.to_a
          if children.any?
            child_with_total_estimated_hours = children.select {|c| c.total_estimated_hours.to_f > 0.0}
            if child_with_total_estimated_hours.any?
              average = Rational(
                child_with_total_estimated_hours.sum(&:total_estimated_hours).to_s,
                child_with_total_estimated_hours.count
              )
            else
              average = Rational(1)
            end
            done = children.sum do |c|
              estimated = Rational(c.total_estimated_hours.to_f.to_s)
              estimated = average unless estimated > 0.0
              ratio = c.closed? ? 100 : (c.done_ratio || 0)
              estimated * ratio
            end
            progress = Rational(done, average * children.count)
            p.done_ratio = progress.floor
          end
        end
      end

      # ancestors will be recursively updated
      p.save(:validate => false)
    end
  end

  # Singleton class method is public
  class << self
    # Update issues so their versions are not pointing to a
    # fixed_version that is not shared with the issue's project
    def update_versions(conditions=nil)
      # Only need to update issues with a fixed_version from
      # a different project and that is not systemwide shared
      Issue.joins(:project, :fixed_version).
        where("#{Issue.table_name}.fixed_version_id IS NOT NULL" +
          " AND #{Issue.table_name}.project_id <> #{::Version.table_name}.project_id" +
          " AND #{::Version.table_name}.sharing <> 'system'").
        where(conditions).each do |issue|
        next if issue.project.nil? || issue.fixed_version.nil?

        unless issue.project.shared_versions.include?(issue.fixed_version)
          retried = false
          begin
            issue.init_journal(User.current)
            issue.fixed_version = nil
            issue.save
          rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError
            raise if retried

            retried = true
            issue.reload
            retry
          end
        end
      end
    end
  end

  def delete_selected_attachments
    if deleted_attachment_ids.present?

# Callback on file attachment
  def attachment_added(attachment)
    if current_journal && !attachment.new_record? && !copy?
      current_journal.journalize_attachment(attachment, :added)
    end
  end

  # Callback on attachment deletion
  def attachment_removed(attachment)
    if current_journal && !attachment.new_record?
      current_journal.journalize_attachment(attachment, :removed)
      current_journal.save
      # Attachment removal via AJAX saves only the journal, so the usual issue update callback does not fire.
      Webhook.trigger(event_name('updated'), self) unless saved_changes?
    end
  end

  # Called after a relation is added
  def relation_added(relation)
    if current_journal
      current_journal.journalize_relation(relation, :added)
      current_journal.save
    end
  end

  # Called after a relation is removed
  def relation_removed(relation)
    if current_journal
      current_journal.journalize_relation(relation, :removed)
      current_journal.save
    end
  end

  # Default assignment based on project or category
  def default_assign
    if assigned_to.nil?
      if category && category.assigned_to
        self.assigned_to = category.assigned_to
      elsif project && project.default_assigned_to
        self.assigned_to = project.default_assigned_to
      end
    end
  end

  # Updates start/due dates of following issues
  def reschedule_following_issues
    if saved_change_to_start_date? || saved_change_to_due_date?
      relations_from.each do |relation|
        relation.set_issue_to_dates(@current_journal)
      end
    end
  end

  # Closes duplicates if the issue is being closed
  def close_duplicates
    if Setting.close_duplicate_issues? && closing?
      duplicates.each do |duplicate|
        # Reload is needed in case the duplicate was updated by a previous duplicate
        duplicate.reload
        # Don't re-close it if it's already closed
        next if duplicate.closed?

        # Same user and notes
        if @current_journal
          duplicate.init_journal(@current_journal.user, @current_journal.notes)
          duplicate.private_notes = @current_journal.private_notes
        end
        duplicate.update_attribute :status, self.status
      end
    end
  end

  # Make sure updated_on is updated when adding a note and set updated_on now
  # so we can set closed_on with the same value on closing
  def force_updated_on_change
    if changed? || (@current_journal && !@current_journal.notes_and_details_empty?)
      self.updated_on = current_time_from_proper_timezone
      if new_record?
        self.created_on = updated_on
      end
    end
  end

  # Callback for setting closed_on when the issue is closed.
  # The closed_on attribute stores the time of the last closing
  # and is preserved when the issue is reopened.
  def update_closed_on
    if closing?
      self.closed_on = updated_on
    end
  end

  # Saves the changes in a Journal
  # Called after_save
  def create_journal
    if current_journal
      current_journal.save
    end
  end

  def create_parent_issue_journal
    return if persisted? && !saved_change_to_parent_id?
    return if destroyed? && @without_nested_set_update

    child_id = self.id
    old_parent_id, new_parent_id =
      if persisted?
        [parent_id_before_last_save, parent_id]
      elsif destroyed?
        [parent_id, nil]
      else
        [nil, parent_id]
      end

    if old_parent_id.present?
      Issue.transaction do
        if old_parent_issue = Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(old_parent_id)
          old_parent_issue.init_journal(User.current)
          old_parent_issue.current_journal.__send__(:add_attribute_detail, 'child_id', child_id, nil)
          old_parent_issue.save
        end
      end
    end

    if new_parent_id.present?
      Issue.transaction do
        if new_parent_issue = Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(new_parent_id)
          new_parent_issue.init_journal(User.current)
          new_parent_issue.current_journal.__send__(:add_attribute_detail, 'child_id', nil, child_id)
          new_parent_issue.save
        end
      end
    end
  end

  def add_auto_watcher
    if author&.active? &&
        author.allowed_to?(:add_issue_watchers, project) &&
        author.pref.auto_watch_on?('issue_created') &&
        self.watcher_user_ids.exclude?(author.id)
      self.set_watcher(author, true)
    end
  end

  def send_notification
    if notify? && Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_added')
      Mailer.deliver_issue_add(self)
    end
  end

  def clear_disabled_fields
    if tracker
      tracker.disabled_core_fields.each do |attribute|
        send :"#{attribute}=", nil
      end

# Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.

module Issue::Webhookable
  extend ActiveSupport::Concern

  def webhook_payload(user, action)
    h = super
    if action == 'updated' && current_journal.present?
      journal = journals.visible(user).find_by_id(current_journal.id)
      if journal.present?
        h[:data][:journal] = journal_payload(journal, user)
        h[:timestamp] = journal.created_on.iso8601
      end
    end
    h
  end

  private

  def journal_payload(journal, user)
    {
      id: journal.id,
      created_on: journal.created_on.iso8601,
      notes: journal.notes,
      user: {
        id: journal.user.id,
        name: journal.user.name,
      },
      details: journal.visible_details(user).map do |d|
        {
          property: d.property,
          prop_key: d.prop_key,
          old_value: d.old_value,
          value: d.value,
        }
      end
    }
  end
end

class AutoCompletesController < ApplicationController
  before_action :find_project

  def issues
    issues = []
    q = (params[:q] || params[:term]).to_s.strip
    status = params[:status].to_s
    issue_id = params[:issue_id].to_s

    end

content.text:
I now have a complete picture. Let me write up the full audit.

---

# Issue Contract Audit — Redmine

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project.** Redmine is a Ruby on Rails project-management / issue-tracking web app (`README.rdoc:1`, license GPL). Standard Rails layout.

**Code organization.**
- `app/models/` — domain models (~70 files). `app/controllers/` (~55), `app/helpers/`, `app/views/`, `app/jobs/` (background jobs via ActiveJob), `app/validators/`.
- `lib/redmine/` — the framework-extension layer: `lib/redmine/acts/` and `lib/plugins/acts_as_*/` hold the reusable "behaviors" mixed into models; `lib/redmine/helpers/` holds Gantt/Calendar/TimeReport renderers; `lib/redmine/nested_set/` the tree logic; `lib/redmine/search.rb`, `field_format.rb`, `menu_manager.rb`, `preparation.rb`.
- `config/`, `db/`, `test/` (unit/functional/integration + `test/fixtures/`), `plugins/`.

**The central record.** `Issue` — `app/models/issue.rb` (2151 lines). Everything in the tracker revolves around it: people create it, comment via journals, log time against it, link it via relations and changesets, nest it as parent/child, and report on it. The shared pieces it is built on:
- **Journal/JournalDetail** (`app/models/journal.rb`, `journal_detail.rb`) — history + comments.
- **TimeEntry** (`app/models/time_entry.rb`) — logged time.
- **IssueRelation** (`app/models/issue_relation.rb`) — links between issues.
- **Changeset** (`app/models/changeset.rb`) — VCS commits referencing issues.
- **Attachment / CustomValue / Watcher / Reaction** — via `acts_as_*` mixins.
- Lookup tables: `IssueStatus`, `Tracker`, `IssuePriority`, `IssueCategory`, `Version`, `Project`, `User`/`Principal`.
- `IssueQuery` (`app/models/issue_query.rb`) — the reporting/filtering engine.

---

## Step 2 — The Issue contract and what it's built on

**Model:** `app/models/issue.rb:20`.

**Shared behaviors (plugin-style mixins it acts as):**
- `include Redmine::SafeAttributes` — `issue.rb:21`, def at `lib/redmine/safe_attributes.rb`; mass-assignment whitelist per-user (`safe_attributes=` `issue.rb:565`).
- `include Redmine::Utils::DateCalculation` — `issue.rb:22` — working-day math for scheduling.
- `include Redmine::I18n` — `issue.rb:23`.
- `include Redmine::NestedSet::IssueNestedSet` — `issue.rb:29`, def `lib/redmine/nested_set/issue_nested_set.rb:22`; adds `before_create/after_create/before_update/before_destroy` tree callbacks (`:27-30`), incl. `destroy_children` (`:134`).
- `include Redmine::Reaction::Reactable` — `issue.rb:30`, def `lib/redmine/reaction.rb:35`; `has_many :reactions, dependent: :delete_all` (`reaction.rb:39`).
- `acts_as_attachable` — `issue.rb:48`, def `lib/plugins/acts_as_attachable/lib/acts_as_attachable.rb:52`; `has_many :attachments, dependent: :destroy` (`:59`), `before_save :attach_saved_attachments` (`:62`). Issue overrides `attachments_editable?` (`issue.rb:218`).
- `acts_as_customizable` — `issue.rb:49`, def `acts_as_customizable.rb:28`; `has_many :custom_values, dependent: :delete_all` (`:33`), `after_save :save_custom_field_values`, `after_destroy :destroy_custom_value_attachments` (`:41-42`).
- `acts_as_watchable` — `issue.rb:50`, def `acts_as_watchable.rb:29`; `has_many :watchers, dependent: :delete_all` + `watcher_users` (`:33-34`).
- `acts_as_searchable` — `issue.rb:51`, def `acts_as_searchable.rb:39`; indexes subject/description, with a visibility `:scope` lambda.
- `acts_as_event` — `issue.rb:55`, def `acts_as_event.rb:28`; activity-feed title/url/type.
- `acts_as_activity_provider` — `issue.rb:59`, def `acts_as_activity_provider.rb:28`.
- `acts_as_mentionable :attributes => ['description']` — `issue.rb:62`, def `lib/redmine/acts/mentionable.rb:28`; `after_save :parse_mentions`.
- `acts_as_webhookable` + `include Issue::Webhookable` — `issue.rb:63-64`, def `lib/redmine/acts/webhookable.rb:28` and `app/models/concerns/issue/webhookable.rb:20`; `after_create_commit/after_update_commit/after_destroy_commit -> Webhook.trigger` (`webhookable.rb:35-39`).

**Associations other records hang off it:**
- `has_many :journals, as: :journalized, dependent: :destroy` — `issue.rb:41`.
- `has_many :time_entries, dependent: :destroy` — `issue.rb:42`.
- `has_and_belongs_to_many :changesets` — `issue.rb:43`.
- `has_many :relations_from / :relations_to (IssueRelation), dependent: :delete_all` — `issue.rb:45-46`.
- belongs_to `project, tracker, status, author, assigned_to(Principal), fixed_version(Version), priority, category` — `issue.rb:32-39`.
- Reverse: `Version#fixed_issues` (`version.rb:127`, `dependent: :nullify`), `IssueCategory#issues` (`issue_category.rb:24`, `:nullify`), `Project#issues` (`project.rb:40`, `:destroy`), `Tracker#issues` (`tracker.rb:33`), `IssuePriority#issues` (`issue_priority.rb:21`), `Journal#issue` (`journal.rb:27`), `TimeEntry#issue` (`time_entry.rb:25`).

**What a teammate must know before changing it:** The destroy semantics are *non-uniform* — journals/time_entries/attachments use `dependent: :destroy` (callbacks fire, slow), relations/custom_values/watchers/reactions use `delete_all` (no callbacks). Children are destroyed via the nested-set `before_destroy :destroy_children` (`issue_nested_set.rb:134`), not Rails' `dependent`. `Version` and `IssueCategory` *nullify* rather than cascade. `destroy` is overridden (`issue.rb:260`) to swallow `StaleObjectError`/`RecordNotFound` during bulk/project teardown. Callback ordering is load-bearing and documented inline (`issue.rb:127-134`).

## Step 3 — What happens on create / update / destroy

**Trigger points (request handling):**
- `IssuesController#create` → `save_issue_with_child_records` — `issues_controller.rb:151`, `:668`.
- `#update` → `save_issue_with_child_records` — `:198`, `:668`; field marshalling in `update_issue_from_params` (`:561`).
- `#bulk_update` — `:349`; `#destroy` — `:431`.
- Mail-in: `MailHandler#receive_issue` (`mail_handler.rb:201`) and `#receive_issue_reply` (`:233`).
- VCS: `Changeset#scan_comment_for_issue_ids` updates/fixes issues (`changeset.rb:215`,`:253`).
- API: `accept_api_auth` on index/show/create/update/destroy (`issues_controller.rb:29`).

**Fan-out on save** (callbacks, mostly **inline** in the save transaction unless noted):
1. `before_validation`: `default_assign` (`issue.rb:1989`), clear disabled fields (`:2105`), force CF defaults (`:2119`).
2. `before_save`: `set_parent_id` (`:1548`); `close_duplicates` (`:2009`, cascades status to duplicates inline); `update_done_ratio_from_issue_status` (`:875`); `force_updated_on_change` (`:2029`); `update_closed_on` (`:2041`).
3. nested-set `before_update :handle_parent_change` (`issue_nested_set.rb:29`).
4. `after_save`: `reschedule_following_issues` (`issue.rb:2000` → IssueRelation#set_issue_to_dates, **inline**); `update_nested_set_attributes` (`:1836`); `update_parent_attributes` → `recalculate_attributes_for` (parent priority/dates/done_ratio rollup, recursive, **inline**) (`:1856`,`:1863`); `delete_selected_attachments` (`:1948`); `create_journal` (`:2049` — persists the history record **inline**).
5. `after_save :after_create_from_copy` (`:1765`); `acts_as_customizable after_save :save_custom_field_values`; `acts_as_mentionable after_save :parse_mentions`.
6. `after_create_commit :send_notification` (`:132`,`:2099`) → `Mailer.deliver_issue_add` → `deliver_later` (**async**, `mailer.rb:108`); `after_create_commit :add_auto_watcher` (`:133`,`:2090`).
7. `after_commit :create_parent_issue_journal` (`:134`,`:2055` — journals the parent's child-add/remove, **inline** in its own transaction).
8. Webhooks: `after_*_commit -> Webhook.trigger` (`webhookable.rb:35-39`) → `WebhookJob.perform_later` (**async**, `webhook.rb:115`).
9. On update, `Journal#after_create_commit :send_notification` (`journal.rb:66`) → `Mailer.deliver_issue_edit` → `deliver_later` (**async**, `mailer.rb:147`).

**Fan-out on destroy** (inline): nested-set `before_destroy :destroy_children` (`issue_nested_set.rb:134`); `dependent: :destroy` → journals (`issue.rb:41`), time_entries (`:42`), attachments; `delete_all` → relations (`:45-46`), custom_values, watchers, reactions; `after_destroy :update_parent_attributes` (`:124`); `after_commit :create_parent_issue_journal` (records child removal); `after_destroy_commit -> Webhook.trigger('deleted')` (**async**). `Version`/`IssueCategory` FKs nullified.

**Attached-data side journaling** (AJAX paths that journal without a full issue save): `attachment_removed` (`issue.rb:1963` — saves journal + fires webhook directly), `relation_added`/`relation_removed` (`:1973`,`:1981`).

## Step 5 — How identity & visibility are guarded

The single source of truth is **`Issue.visible_condition(user, options)`** (SQL) — `issue.rb:137` — and the instance **`Issue#visible?(user)`** — `issue.rb:172`. Both branch on `Role#issues_visibility` (`all`/`default`/`own`), `is_private`, author/assignee identity, and per-tracker `view_issues` permission (`:159-166`,`:189-191`). These delegate to `Project.allowed_to_condition` (`project.rb`).

The `scope :visible` (`issue.rb:84`) wraps `visible_condition` and is the guard used by background/cross-subsystem callers:
- **Autocomplete:** `Issue.cross_project_scope(...).visible` — `auto_completes_controller.rb:29`.
- **Quick-jump / search:** `Issue.visible.find_by_id` — `search_controller.rb:41`; `acts_as_searchable` applies `.visible(user)` in `search_scope` (`acts_as_searchable.rb:198`), invoked by `lib/redmine/search.rb:118`.
- **Wiki macros / autolinks:** `Issue.visible.find_by` — `macros.rb:368`; `application_helper.rb:1195`.
- **Gantt / Calendar:** go through `@query.issues(...)` where `IssueQuery` applies the visible base scope — `gantt.rb:160`; `IssueQuery` uses `Issue.visible_condition` even for subtask subqueries (`issue_query.rb:62`).
- **Repository/changeset linking:** `Issue.visible.find_by_id` — `repositories_controller.rb:265`, `previews_controller.rb:25`.
- **Time entries:** the `visible` scope LEFT-JOINs issues with `Issue.visible_condition(User.current)` — `time_entry.rb:68-70`; spent-hours rollups use `load_visible_spent_hours` (`issue.rb:1241`).
- **Journals/history:** `Journal.visible` re-applies `Issue.visible_condition` (`journal.rb:73`) and detail-level checks call `.visible?` (`journal.rb:127`); `Issue#visible_journals_with_index` filters private notes (`issue.rb:924`).
- **Email (background):** `Mailer.reminders` re-checks per recipient — `issues.select {|i| i.visible?(assignee)}` (`mailer.rb:637`); recipient lists come from `issue.notified_users`/`notified_watchers`/`notified_mentions` (`mailer.rb:106`), which themselves filter by visibility (`issue.rb:1148`).
- **Reactions:** `Redmine::Reaction.visible?/editable?` gate display (`reaction.rb:26,:31`).
- **Identity guard for actor context:** `User.current` is swapped per-recipient inside `Mailer.with_synched_deliveries`/loop (`mailer.rb:47-58`) so visibility is evaluated as the *recipient*, not the actor.

Edit/act guards (beyond view): `attributes_editable?` (`issue.rb:207`), `notes_addable?` (`:223`), `deletable?` (`:228`), `attachments_editable?` (`:218`), `time_loggable?` (`:197`), plus controller `before_action :authorize` (`issues_controller.rb:25`).

## Step 6 — Blast radius of the contract change (storage + teardown of attached data)

Ranked by risk for a change to *how attached data is stored and torn down*:

**Critical (silent data loss / orphans if cascade semantics change):**
1. `issue.rb:41-46` — the `dependent:` declarations themselves (journals/time_entries `:destroy`; relations `:delete_all`). Changing any cascade strategy directly changes teardown.
2. `lib/redmine/nested_set/issue_nested_set.rb:134` `destroy_children` + `destroy_without_nested_set_update` (`:154`) — children are torn down here, *not* via `dependent`. Easy to miss; governs project-delete and parent-delete (`destroy_project_job.rb`).
3. `version.rb:127` (`:nullify`) and `issue_category.rb:24` (`:nullify`) — reverse FKs that must stay consistent; a switch to destroy/restrict here changes behavior on version/category deletion.
4. `issue.rb:260 destroy` override (Stale/NotFound rescue) — bulk delete and `Project#destroy` (`project.rb:40`) rely on it; breaking it pages on project teardown.
5. `acts_as_customizable` `delete_all` of custom_values + `destroy_custom_value_attachments` after_destroy (`acts_as_customizable.rb:33,:42`) — attachment-backed custom field values can orphan files.
6. `acts_as_attachable` `dependent: :destroy` (`acts_as_attachable.rb:59`) + `delete_selected_attachments` (`issue.rb:1948`) and the AJAX `attachment_removed` journaling path (`issue.rb:1963`).

**High (behavioral breakage):**
7. Parent rollup `recalculate_attributes_for` (`issue.rb:1863`) + `after_destroy :update_parent_attributes` (`:124`) — done_ratio/dates/priority of parents recompute on child teardown.
8. `create_parent_issue_journal` (`issue.rb:2055`) — journals child add/remove on the parent; runs on destroy too.
9. Journal cascade + `Journal.visible` (`journal.rb:73`) and `JournalDetail dependent: :delete_all` (`journal.rb:31`) — history teardown + visibility.
10. Webhooks `after_destroy_commit -> Webhook.trigger('deleted')` (`webhookable.rb:39`) — external systems depend on the deleted payload (`concerns/issue/webhookable.rb`).
11. `time_entry.rb:25` + `time_entry.rb:68-70` visible join + `time_entry_query.rb` — if time entries no longer cascade, reports/joins must handle orphans.
12. `IssueRelation` `delete_all` (`issue.rb:45-46`) + relation-removed journaling (`issue_relation.rb:80`) + reschedule dependents (`issue.rb:2000`).

**Medium:**
13. `changeset.rb` HABTM `changesets` join rows (`issue.rb:43`) — join-table cleanup on issue delete.
14. `acts_as_watchable`/`Reactable` `delete_all` (`acts_as_watchable.rb:33`, `reaction.rb:39`).
15. `acts_as_searchable` index + `acts_as_mentionable` — stale search/mention rows after teardown.
16. `IssueImport`/`issue_import.rb`, `mail_handler.rb` — creation paths must still satisfy the contract.

**Lower (read-side, re-verify queries don't assume presence):** reporting (`reports_controller.rb`, `Issue.by_*`), gantt/calendar (`gantt.rb`, `my_helper.rb`), version/project/user counts (`projects_controller.rb:186`, `users_controller.rb:101`, `version.rb:256`).

**Must re-verify after the change:** project deletion (`DestroyProjectJob`), bulk issue delete (`issues_controller.rb:431`), parent/child rollups, version/category nullify, webhook `deleted` payloads, time-entry & journal visibility joins, and search index consistency.

# Issue Contract Audit — Redmine

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project.** Redmine is a Ruby on Rails project-management / issue-tracking web app (`README.rdoc:1`, license GPL). Standard Rails layout.

**Code organization.**
- `app/models/` — domain models (~70 files). `app/controllers/` (~55), `app/helpers/`, `app/views/`, `app/jobs/` (background jobs via ActiveJob), `app/validators/`.
- `lib/redmine/` — the framework-extension layer: `lib/redmine/acts/` and `lib/plugins/acts_as_*/` hold the reusable "behaviors" mixed into models; `lib/redmine/helpers/` holds Gantt/Calendar/TimeReport renderers; `lib/redmine/nested_set/` the tree logic; `lib/redmine/search.rb`, `field_format.rb`, `menu_manager.rb`, `preparation.rb`.
- `config/`, `db/`, `test/` (unit/functional/integration + `test/fixtures/`), `plugins/`.

**The central record.** `Issue` — `app/models/issue.rb` (2151 lines). Everything in the tracker revolves around it: people create it, comment via journals, log time against it, link it via relations and changesets, nest it as parent/child, and report on it. The shared pieces it is built on:
- **Journal/JournalDetail** (`app/models/journal.rb`, `journal_detail.rb`) — history + comments.
- **TimeEntry** (`app/models/time_entry.rb`) — logged time.
- **IssueRelation** (`app/models/issue_relation.rb`) — links between issues.
- **Changeset** (`app/models/changeset.rb`) — VCS commits referencing issues.
- **Attachment / CustomValue / Watcher / Reaction** — via `acts_as_*` mixins.
- Lookup tables: `IssueStatus`, `Tracker`, `IssuePriority`, `IssueCategory`, `Version`, `Project`, `User`/`Principal`.
- `IssueQuery` (`app/models/issue_query.rb`) — the reporting/filtering engine.

---

## Step 2 — The Issue contract and what it's built on

**Model:** `app/models/issue.rb:20`.

**Shared behaviors (plugin-style mixins it acts as):**
- `include Redmine::SafeAttributes` — `issue.rb:21`, def at `lib/redmine/safe_attributes.rb`; mass-assignment whitelist per-user (`safe_attributes=` `issue.rb:565`).
- `include Redmine::Utils::DateCalculation` — `issue.rb:22` — working-day math for scheduling.
- `include Redmine::I18n` — `issue.rb:23`.
- `include Redmine::NestedSet::IssueNestedSet` — `issue.rb:29`, def `lib/redmine/nested_set/issue_nested_set.rb:22`; adds `before_create/after_create/before_update/before_destroy` tree callbacks (`:27-30`), incl. `destroy_children` (`:134`).
- `include Redmine::Reaction::Reactable` — `issue.rb:30`, def `lib/redmine/reaction.rb:35`; `has_many :reactions, dependent: :delete_all` (`reaction.rb:39`).
- `acts_as_attachable` — `issue.rb:48`, def `lib/plugins/acts_as_attachable/lib/acts_as_attachable.rb:52`; `has_many :attachments, dependent: :destroy` (`:59`), `before_save :attach_saved_attachments` (`:62`). Issue overrides `attachments_editable?` (`issue.rb:218`).
- `acts_as_customizable` — `issue.rb:49`, def `acts_as_customizable.rb:28`; `has_many :custom_values, dependent: :delete_all` (`:33`), `after_save :save_custom_field_values`, `after_destroy :destroy_custom_value_attachments` (`:41-42`).
- `acts_as_watchable` — `issue.rb:50`, def `acts_as_watchable.rb:29`; `has_many :watchers, dependent: :delete_all` + `watcher_users` (`:33-34`).
- `acts_as_searchable` — `issue.rb:51`, def `acts_as_searchable.rb:39`; indexes subject/description, with a visibility `:scope` lambda.
- `acts_as_event` — `issue.rb:55`, def `acts_as_event.rb:28`; activity-feed title/url/type.
- `acts_as_activity_provider` — `issue.rb:59`, def `acts_as_activity_provider.rb:28`.
- `acts_as_mentionable :attributes => ['description']` — `issue.rb:62`, def `lib/redmine/acts/mentionable.rb:28`; `after_save :parse_mentions`.
- `acts_as_webhookable` + `include Issue::Webhookable` — `issue.rb:63-64`, def `lib/redmine/acts/webhookable.rb:28` and `app/models/concerns/issue/webhookable.rb:20`; `after_create_commit/after_update_commit/after_destroy_commit -> Webhook.trigger` (`webhookable.rb:35-39`).

**Associations other records hang off it:**
- `has_many :journals, as: :journalized, dependent: :destroy` — `issue.rb:41`.
- `has_many :time_entries, dependent: :destroy` — `issue.rb:42`.
- `has_and_belongs_to_many :changesets` — `issue.rb:43`.
- `has_many :relations_from / :relations_to (IssueRelation), dependent: :delete_all` — `issue.rb:45-46`.
- belongs_to `project, tracker, status, author, assigned_to(Principal), fixed_version(Version), priority, category` — `issue.rb:32-39`.
- Reverse: `Version#fixed_issues` (`version.rb:127`, `dependent: :nullify`), `IssueCategory#issues` (`issue_category.rb:24`, `:nullify`), `Project#issues` (`project.rb:40`, `:destroy`), `Tracker#issues` (`tracker.rb:33`), `IssuePriority#issues` (`issue_priority.rb:21`), `Journal#issue` (`journal.rb:27`), `TimeEntry#issue` (`time_entry.rb:25`).

**What a teammate must know before changing it:** The destroy semantics are *non-uniform* — journals/time_entries/attachments use `dependent: :destroy` (callbacks fire, slow), relations/custom_values/watchers/reactions use `delete_all` (no callbacks). Children are destroyed via the nested-set `before_destroy :destroy_children` (`issue_nested_set.rb:134`), not Rails' `dependent`. `Version` and `IssueCategory` *nullify* rather than cascade. `destroy` is overridden (`issue.rb:260`) to swallow `StaleObjectError`/`RecordNotFound` during bulk/project teardown. Callback ordering is load-bearing and documented inline (`issue.rb:127-134`).

---

## Step 3 — What happens on create / update / destroy

**Trigger points (request handling):**
- `IssuesController#create` → `save_issue_with_child_records` — `issues_controller.rb:151`, `:668`.
- `#update` → `save_issue_with_child_records` — `:198`, `:668`; field marshalling in `update_issue_from_params` (`:561`).
- `#bulk_update` — `:349`; `#destroy` — `:431`.
- Mail-in: `MailHandler#receive_issue` (`mail_handler.rb:201`) and `#receive_issue_reply` (`:233`).
- VCS: `Changeset#scan_comment_for_issue_ids` updates/fixes issues (`changeset.rb:215`,`:253`).
- API: `accept_api_auth` on index/show/create/update/destroy (`issues_controller.rb:29`).

**Fan-out on save** (callbacks, mostly **inline** in the save transaction unless noted):
1. `before_validation`: `default_assign` (`issue.rb:1989`), clear disabled fields (`:2105`), force CF defaults (`:2119`).
2. `before_save`: `set_parent_id` (`:1548`); `close_duplicates` (`:2009`, cascades status to duplicates inline); `update_done_ratio_from_issue_status` (`:875`); `force_updated_on_change` (`:2029`); `update_closed_on` (`:2041`).
3. nested-set `before_update :handle_parent_change` (`issue_nested_set.rb:29`).
4. `after_save`: `reschedule_following_issues` (`issue.rb:2000` → IssueRelation#set_issue_to_dates, **inline**); `update_nested_set_attributes` (`:1836`); `update_parent_attributes` → `recalculate_attributes_for` (parent priority/dates/done_ratio rollup, recursive, **inline**) (`:1856`,`:1863`); `delete_selected_attachments` (`:1948`); `create_journal` (`:2049` — persists the history record **inline**).
5. `after_save :after_create_from_copy` (`:1765`); `acts_as_customizable after_save :save_custom_field_values`; `acts_as_mentionable after_save :parse_mentions`.
6. `after_create_commit :send_notification` (`:132`,`:2099`) → `Mailer.deliver_issue_add` → `deliver_later` (**async**, `mailer.rb:108`); `after_create_commit :add_auto_watcher` (`:133`,`:2090`).
7. `after_commit :create_parent_issue_journal` (`:134`,`:2055` — journals the parent's child-add/remove, **inline** in its own transaction).
8. Webhooks: `after_*_commit -> Webhook.trigger` (`webhookable.rb:35-39`) → `WebhookJob.perform_later` (**async**, `webhook.rb:115`).
9. On update, `Journal#after_create_commit :send_notification` (`journal.rb:66`) → `Mailer.deliver_issue_edit` → `deliver_later` (**async**, `mailer.rb:147`).

**Fan-out on destroy** (inline): nested-set `before_destroy :destroy_children` (`issue_nested_set.rb:134`); `dependent: :destroy` → journals (`issue.rb:41`), time_entries (`:42`), attachments; `delete_all` → relations (`:45-46`), custom_values, watchers, reactions; `after_destroy :update_parent_attributes` (`:124`); `after_commit :create_parent_issue_journal` (records child removal); `after_destroy_commit -> Webhook.trigger('deleted')` (**async**). `Version`/`IssueCategory` FKs nullified.

**Attached-data side journaling** (AJAX paths that journal without a full issue save): `attachment_removed` (`issue.rb:1963` — saves journal + fires webhook directly), `relation_added`/`relation_removed` (`:1973`,`:1981`).

### A. Request handling (controllers)

## Step 5 — How identity & visibility are guarded

The single source of truth is **`Issue.visible_condition(user, options)`** (SQL) — `issue.rb:137` — and the instance **`Issue#visible?(user)`** — `issue.rb:172`. Both branch on `Role#issues_visibility` (`all`/`default`/`own`), `is_private`, author/assignee identity, and per-tracker `view_issues` permission (`:159-166`,`:189-191`). These delegate to `Project.allowed_to_condition` (`project.rb`).

The `scope :visible` (`issue.rb:84`) wraps `visible_condition` and is the guard used by background/cross-subsystem callers:
- **Autocomplete:** `Issue.cross_project_scope(...).visible` — `auto_completes_controller.rb:29`.
- **Quick-jump / search:** `Issue.visible.find_by_id` — `search_controller.rb:41`; `acts_as_searchable` applies `.visible(user)` in `search_scope` (`acts_as_searchable.rb:198`), invoked by `lib/redmine/search.rb:118`.
- **Wiki macros / autolinks:** `Issue.visible.find_by` — `macros.rb:368`; `application_helper.rb:1195`.
- **Gantt / Calendar:** go through `@query.issues(...)` where `IssueQuery` applies the visible base scope — `gantt.rb:160`; `IssueQuery` uses `Issue.visible_condition` even for subtask subqueries (`issue_query.rb:62`).
- **Repository/changeset linking:** `Issue.visible.find_by_id` — `repositories_controller.rb:265`, `previews_controller.rb:25`.
- **Time entries:** the `visible` scope LEFT-JOINs issues with `Issue.visible_condition(User.current)` — `time_entry.rb:68-70`; spent-hours rollups use `load_visible_spent_hours` (`issue.rb:1241`).
- **Journals/history:** `Journal.visible` re-applies `Issue.visible_condition` (`journal.rb:73`) and detail-level checks call `.visible?` (`journal.rb:127`); `Issue#visible_journals_with_index` filters private notes (`issue.rb:924`).
- **Email (background):** `Mailer.reminders` re-checks per recipient — `issues.select {|i| i.visible?(assignee)}` (`mailer.rb:637`); recipient lists come from `issue.notified_users`/`notified_watchers`/`notified_mentions` (`mailer.rb:106`), which themselves filter by visibility (`issue.rb:1148`).
- **Reactions:** `Redmine::Reaction.visible?/editable?` gate display (`reaction.rb:26,:31`).
- **Identity guard for actor context:** `User.current` is swapped per-recipient inside `Mailer.with_synched_deliveries`/loop (`mailer.rb:47-58`) so visibility is evaluated as the *recipient*, not the actor.

Edit/act guards (beyond view): `attributes_editable?` (`issue.rb:207`), `notes_addable?` (`:223`), `deletable?` (`:228`), `attachments_editable?` (`:218`), `time_loggable?` (`:197`), plus controller `before_action :authorize` (`issues_controller.rb:25`).

## Step 6 — Blast radius of the contract change (storage + teardown of attached data)

Ranked by risk for a change to *how attached data is stored and torn down*:

**Critical (silent data loss / orphans if cascade semantics change):**
1. `issue.rb:41-46` — the `dependent:` declarations themselves (journals/time_entries `:destroy`; relations `:delete_all`). Changing any cascade strategy directly changes teardown.
2. `lib/redmine/nested_set/issue_nested_set.rb:134` `destroy_children` + `destroy_without_nested_set_update` (`:154`) — children are torn down here, *not* via `dependent`. Easy to miss; governs project-delete and parent-delete (`destroy_project_job.rb`).
3. `version.rb:127` (`:nullify`) and `issue_category.rb:24` (`:nullify`) — reverse FKs that must stay consistent; a switch to destroy/restrict here changes behavior on version/category deletion.
4. `issue.rb:260 destroy` override (Stale/NotFound rescue) — bulk delete and `Project#destroy` (`project.rb:40`) rely on it; breaking it pages on project teardown.
5. `acts_as_customizable` `delete_all` of custom_values + `destroy_custom_value_attachments` after_destroy (`acts_as_customizable.rb:33,:42`) — attachment-backed custom field values can orphan files.
6. `acts_as_attachable` `dependent: :destroy` (`acts_as_attachable.rb:59`) + `delete_selected_attachments` (`issue.rb:1948`) and the AJAX `attachment_removed` journaling path (`issue.rb:1963`).

**High (behavioral breakage):**
7. Parent rollup `recalculate_attributes_for` (`issue.rb:1863`) + `after_destroy :update_parent_attributes` (`:124`) — done_ratio/dates/priority of parents recompute on child teardown.
8. `create_parent_issue_journal` (`issue.rb:2055`) — journals child add/remove on the parent; runs on destroy too.
9. Journal cascade + `Journal.visible` (`journal.rb:73`) and `JournalDetail dependent: :delete_all` (`journal.rb:31`) — history teardown + visibility.
10. Webhooks `after_destroy_commit -> Webhook.trigger('deleted')` (`webhookable.rb:39`) — external systems depend on the deleted payload (`concerns/issue/webhookable.rb`).
11. `time_entry.rb:25` + `time_entry.rb:68-70` visible join + `time_entry_query.rb` — if time entries no longer cascade, reports/joins must handle orphans.
12. `IssueRelation` `delete_all` (`issue.rb:45-46`) + relation-removed journaling (`issue_relation.rb:80`) + reschedule dependents (`issue.rb:2000`).

**Medium:**
13. `changeset.rb` HABTM `changesets` join rows (`issue.rb:43`) — join-table cleanup on issue delete.
14. `acts_as_watchable`/`Reactable` `delete_all` (`acts_as_watchable.rb:33`, `reaction.rb:39`).
15. `acts_as_searchable` index + `acts_as_mentionable` — stale search/mention rows after teardown.
16. `IssueImport`/`issue_import.rb`, `mail_handler.rb` — creation paths must still satisfy the contract.

**Lower (read-side, re-verify queries don't assume presence):** reporting (`reports_controller.rb`, `Issue.by_*`), gantt/calendar (`gantt.rb`, `my_helper.rb`), version/project/user counts (`projects_controller.rb:186`, `users_controller.rb:101`, `version.rb:256`).

**Must re-verify after the change:** project deletion (`DestroyProjectJob`), bulk issue delete (`issues_controller.rb:431`), parent/child rollups, version/category nullify, webhook `deleted` payloads, time-entry & journal visibility joins, and search index consistency.
